title: Argo CD API RBAC Violation Attempts — Unauthorized Sync and Delete
dataSources: `index=devops` `sourcetype=argocd:server` API audit lines (403/Forbidden, permission denied, RBAC decision)
app: Splunk Add-on for Argo CD, Argo CD `--enable-grpc-web` audit middleware or proxy logs
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="argocd:server"
| search "403" OR "Forbidden" OR "permission denied" OR "not allowed" OR "RBAC"
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?P<verb>sync|delete|update|patch|create).*application\\s+(?P<app>[^\\s]+)"
| stats count, values(user) as users, values(remote_ip) as src_ips by app, verb
| where count >= 5
| sort -count
description: Repeated denied sync or delete attempts often precede insider misuse, stolen tokens, or automation using the wrong service account. Centralizing Argo CD API denials gives security teams a high-fidelity signal without waiting for Kubernetes audit noise.
value: Supports least-privilege enforcement and proves detective control coverage for GitOps platforms under SOC2 or internal SRE policy.
implementation: Confirm API server logs include authenticated user or OIDC subject and source IP. Add allowlists for known CI service accounts. Forward to a security index with stricter retention; pair with SOAR for automatic ticket creation on novel user+app pairs.
visualization: Bar chart (denials by user), heatmap (user × application), timeline with drilldown to raw log lines.
grandmaExplanation: We track when someone tries to change or remove deployments but is told they are not allowed. A burst of those tries can mean a stolen password or a broken script, so we surface it quickly.
